quizzes.lessonTitle: When to change the plan, and closing the job
quizzes.title: Applied Structural Drying Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: What is the "dry standard" used to set a drying goal?
options: A fixed moisture percentage that applies to every building
The moisture reading of the same or similar material in an unaffected part of the building
The reading on the day the equipment is removed
The manufacturer’s rated output for the dehumidifier

questions.questionText: Why can relative humidity (RH) alone be misleading when judging how much water is in the air?
options: RH is only measured in the United States
RH cannot be measured with a hygrometer
RH depends on temperature, so the same amount of water shows a different RH as the air warms or cools
RH is always higher than the dew point

questions.questionText: In Australian practice, specific humidity (the actual mass of water in the air) is expressed in:
options: Relative humidity as a percentage (%)
Grams of water per kilogram of dry air (g/kg)
Litres per square metre
Degrees Celsius (°C)

questions.questionText: What is the main job of an air mover in structural drying?
options: To remove water from the air
To heat the structure to boiling point
To move air across wet surfaces, breaking up the moist boundary layer so evaporation continues
To replace the need for a dehumidifier

questions.questionText: The drying space is warm and clammy and windows are beginning to sweat. What does this most likely indicate?
options: Drying is finished and equipment can be removed
There is more evaporation (airflow) than the dehumidification can remove, so add dehumidification or reduce airflow
The dry standard was set too low
The air movers should all be switched off permanently

questions.questionText: Which dehumidifier type generally keeps working best in cool and already-dry air where refrigerant units fade?
options: Conventional refrigerant
Desiccant
A pedestal fan
None — all stop working when cool

questions.questionText: A standard Australian general power outlet (GPO) is rated at:
options: 15 A at 230 V
10 A at 230 V
20 A at 250 V
32 A at 400 V three-phase

questions.questionText: When budgeting electrical load for a drying setup on an Australian site, you should:
options: Use overseas equipment guides that quote current at a lower mains voltage without adjustment
Assume every power outlet is on its own independent circuit
Add up each machine’s running current at 230 V, keep the total on any circuit below its rating, and spread machines across circuits
Run all machines through a single daisy-chained power board

questions.questionText: Visible mould is present on a water-damaged wall. What is the correct approach to airflow?
options: Aim air movers directly at the mould to dry it faster
Do not run air movers across affected surfaces; contain, filter (AFDs) and dehumidify first, and introduce air movers only after the area is cleaned and cleared
Increase air mover speed to blow the spores outside
Ignore the mould and continue normal drying

questions.questionText: Material moisture readings have stopped falling for more than a day. The best response is to:
options: Simply wait several more days without changing anything
Declare the job complete because the readings are stable
Investigate the cause (chamber leaking, dehumidification not keeping up, airflow not reaching a wet area, or a hidden water reservoir) and change the drying design
Remove all equipment and issue the certificate
